methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $140M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $1.5B - $4.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 7.4x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Series C), post-money $4B

private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Series C), post-money $2.4B

private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Series C), post-money $1.5B

data.narrative:
Aleph Farms is a Series C-stage biotech company that has raised $140M+ to date. Valuation analysis draws on a peer set of 3 public-market biotech comparables (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private companies in the same sector, with 3 same-stage private peers forming the primary anchor: Ultima Genomics ($4.0B), Insitro ($2.4B), and Colossal Biosciences ($1.5B).

Two triangulation methods are applicable. The stage-progression method applies a typical 1.7x step-up multiplier to the $140M capital raised proxy, implying a $238M valuation. The peer-median method anchors to the three same-stage comparables, yielding a $2.4B implied valuation at the median, with a range of $1.5B–$4.0B. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied due to lack of revenue disclosure, a standard constraint for private biotech.

The triangulated range is $202M (low) to $2.8B (high), with a $1.3B median across the two applicable methods. The substantial width reflects material tension between capital-raised-based approaches and peer-median valuation. Key uncertainties include the company's revenue trajectory relative to peers, stage-appropriate risk adjustment, and whether peer valuations reflect current market conditions or reflect prior-round pricing momentum. Further clarity on commercialization milestones and burn rate would narrow this range.
